title: Deep Learning for Climate Model Emulation
description: This dataset contains training data and pre-trained models for emulating climate models using deep learning techniques. Includes temperature, precipitation, and atmospheric composition data from CMIP6 simulations.
creators: Emma Strubell; ; Carnegie Mellon University | Anand Gnanadesikan; ; Johns Hopkins University
contributors: David Rolnick; McGill University; projectmember | Yoshua Bengio; ; supervisor
subjects: climate change;deep learning;machine learning;earth system models;climate modeling
additional_descriptions: Dataset derived from CMIP6 climate model outputs with standardized preprocessing;methods | Neural network architectures include CNNs and Transformers for spatiotemporal modeling;technical-info
references: Kashinath, K. et al. (2021). Physics-informed machine learning. Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society A; Reichstein, M. et al. (2019). Deep learning and process understanding for data-driven Earth system science. Nature.

title: COVID-19 Open Research Dataset
description: A resource of over 1 million scholarly articles about COVID-19, SARS-CoV-2, and related coronaviruses. Includes full text, metadata, and embeddings for natural language processing research.
creators: Lucy Lu Wang; ; University of Washington | Kyle Lo; ; Allen Institute for AI
contributors: Doug Downey; Northwestern University; datacurator | Sergey Feldman; ; researcher
subjects: covid-19;natural language processing;biomedical text mining;machine learning;information extraction
additional_descriptions: Text extracted from PubMed Central, preprint servers, and other sources;methods | Embeddings generated using SciBERT and SPECTER models;technical-info | Updated weekly with new publications through August 2023;other
references: Wang, L.L. et al. (2020). CORD-19: The COVID-19 Open Research Dataset. Proceedings of NLP-COVID Workshop; Lo, K. et al. (2020). S2ORC: The Semantic Scholar Open Research Corpus. ACL.

title: Cancer Genome Atlas Pan-Cancer Data
description: Comprehensive multi-omics data from 33 cancer types including genomic, transcriptomic, proteomic, and clinical data from over 11,000 patients. Essential resource for cancer research.
creators: John N. Weinstein; ; University of Texas MD Anderson | Katherine A. Hoadley; ; University of North Carolina
contributors: Charles M. Perou; UNC Chapel Hill; researcher | Matthew Meyerson; ; projectmember
additional_descriptions: Data generated using RNA-seq, whole exome sequencing, and reverse phase protein arrays;technical-info | Standardized processing pipelines ensure data consistency across cancer types;methods | Clinical outcomes include survival, treatment response, and tumor staging;other
references: Weinstein, J.N. et al. (2013). The Cancer Genome Atlas Pan-Cancer analysis project. Nature Genetics; Hutter, C. et al. (2018). The Cancer Genome Atlas: Creating Lasting Value beyond Its Data. Cell.

title: AlphaFold Protein Structure Database
description: Predicted 3D structures of proteins from across the tree of life, generated using the AlphaFold2 deep learning system. Contains over 200 million protein structure predictions.
creators: John Jumper; ; DeepMind | Demis Hassabis; ; DeepMind
contributors: Kathryn Tunyasuvunakool; DeepMind; researcher | Pushmeet Kohli; ; projectmanager
additional_descriptions: Structures predicted using AlphaFold2 with per-residue confidence scores (pLDDT);technical-info | Covers proteomes from model organisms and important pathogens;methods | Updated quarterly with improved predictions and expanded coverage;other
references: Jumper, J. et al. (2021). Highly accurate protein structure prediction with AlphaFold. Nature; Varadi, M. et al. (2022). AlphaFold Protein Structure Database. Nucleic Acids Research.

title: Astrophysical Multimessenger Observatory Network Alerts
description: Real-time alerts from high-energy astrophysical events combining data from neutrino telescopes, gravitational wave detectors, and gamma-ray observatories. Critical for multi-messenger astronomy.
creators: Hugo Ayala; ; Pennsylvania State University | Azadeh Keivani; ; Columbia University
contributors: Derek Fox; Penn State; projectmember | Marcos Santander; ; datacollector
additional_descriptions: Alerts generated through coincidence analysis across multiple observatories;methods | Sub-second latency for real-time follow-up observations;technical-info | Includes event localization, energy estimates, and significance values;other
references: Ayala Solares, H.A. et al. (2020). The Astrophysical Multimessenger Observatory Network (AMON). Astroparticle Physics; Abbott, B.P. et al. (2017). Multi-messenger Observations of a Binary Neutron Star Merger. ApJL.
